name: Missouri ITSD (OA Information Technology Services Division)
tagline: Missouri's consolidated executive-branch IT: ITSD, inside the Office of Administration, builds, secures, and operates technology for the state's IT-consolidated executive departments — and the OA Division of Purchasing runs the statewide contracts most Missouri public buyers use.
overview: The Information Technology Services Division (ITSD) sits within Missouri's Office of Administration (OA) and is the state's consolidated executive-branch IT organization. A team of roughly 1,000 IT professionals delivers client engagement, information security, networks and telecommunications, and web/application development across about 15 IT-consolidated executive departments (Agriculture, Corrections, Education, Health & Senior Services, and others), plus the Governor's and Lt. Governor's offices. Consolidation is not total: the Departments of Conservation and Transportation run their own IT, and several statewide elected offices (Attorney General, Auditor, Secretary of State, Treasurer) operate independently — so ITSD is the central executive-branch buyer and standards layer, not a single front door for all of Missouri government. Procurement is a separate OA function: the Division of Purchasing establishes statewide contracts through MissouriBUYS and extends them to local governments via the Cooperative Procurement Program. For a Dell SLG seller, ITSD is both a large enterprise IT buyer and the entity whose standards, security gates, and refresh cycles agency-level deals must align to — set against a fast-moving Missouri AI and data-center backdrop (the $15B Google New Florence project and a new statewide AI executive order).

budget.intro: ITSD's technology spend is appropriated through the Office of Administration in Missouri's annual budget (state fiscal year begins July 1). ITSD is best read as a delivery-and-standards layer: much of the real buying power sits in the consolidated departments' IT lines that ITSD supports, plus the OA digital-government and infrastructure appropriations. Treat the figures below as directional public budget signals, not internal account data.

stats.label: ITSD IT staff
stats.note: IT professionals delivering consolidated executive-branch services

stats.label: Digital-government line (FY2026 EB)
stats.note: OA funding cited for agency-supported maintenance of digital-government transformation systems; confirm scope against the appropriation bill

stats.label: Google New Florence build
stats.note: Private investment, not a state IT budget — but it reshapes Missouri's AI/data-center and energy context

notes: Missouri appropriates IT through the Office of Administration in HB 2-13 / HB 17-20 bills each fiscal year — confirm the exact ITSD line and any FY2027 governor's recommendation against the published budget documents before forecasting.
Because consolidated departments retain their own IT appropriations, much of the addressable spend lives in those agency budgets that ITSD delivers against, not in a single ITSD wallet.

Federal State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP) funding flows through Missouri's Dept. of Public Safety (the State Administrative Agency), with the bulk passed to local governments — a separate, grant-funded security pool adjacent to ITSD's enterprise work.

initiatives.name: Statewide AI strategic framework (EO 26-02)
initiatives.summary: Governor Kehoe's Executive Order 26-02 (signed Jan 14, 2026) directs OA Commissioner Ken Zellers to build a strategic framework for AI integration across Missouri government — covering service delivery, data privacy/security, human-in-the-loop decision-making, transparency, and data quality — with findings due to the Governor by Nov 30, 2026. ITSD is the natural delivery arm. Position AI-ready infrastructure, compute, and secure data platforms behind responsible-AI adoption.

initiatives.name: Data-center & energy context (Google New Florence)
initiatives.summary: Google's ~$15B data-center investment in New Florence (Montgomery County), code-named Project Spade, is the largest single private investment in state history and sits beside an Amazon-committed site. Paired with SB 4 (signed Apr 9, 2025), which makes large-load data centers pay 100% of their power and infrastructure costs, it positions Missouri as an AI-infrastructure hub. Relevant as market context and a state energy/policy priority rather than an ITSD purchase — useful framing for AI-infrastructure conversations.

initiatives.name: Enterprise cybersecurity & whole-of-state posture
initiatives.summary: ITSD's Information Security office runs executive-branch security; statewide, federal SLCGP grants (administered by the Dept. of Public Safety) push funding to local governments and schools. A combined enterprise-plus-grant security landscape that reaches beyond state agencies into county, city, and K-12 buyers.

initiatives.name: Infrastructure, network & end-user modernization
initiatives.summary: ITSD owns networks and telecommunications and end-user support across the consolidated departments — a recurring line for server, storage, networking (ISG) and client-device (CSG) refresh in a single, centralized buying environment. Map refresh cycles to where ITSD centralizes enterprise infrastructure.

procurement.intro: Selling to ITSD and the broader Missouri public sector runs through the OA Division of Purchasing, not one-off agency RFPs. Statewide contracts are established competitively and published on the MissouriBUYS Contract Board; eligible locals buy off them via the Cooperative Procurement Program. Two things to confirm first: that Dell holds the relevant Missouri statewide / NASPO ValuePoint coverage, and that Dell is registered and current in MissouriBUYS.

vehicles.name: MissouriBUYS statewide contracts
vehicles.detail: Missouri's secure, web-based eProcurement system and Contract Board. 150+ statewide contract categories (650+ individual contracts) cover commodities, services, and technology — the primary path for IT hardware, software, and services to executive-branch agencies.

vehicles.name: Cooperative Procurement Program
vehicles.detail: Statutory program letting Missouri universities, local governments, political subdivisions, and quasi-public bodies buy off state statewide contracts — extending Dell's Missouri coverage well beyond ITSD into the local market.

vehicles.name: NASPO ValuePoint cooperative contracts
vehicles.detail: Missouri participates in NASPO ValuePoint cooperative categories (e.g., Computer Equipment, Cloud Solutions). Buying typically requires a Missouri participating addendum — confirm Dell's PA coverage before pursuing cloud / as-a-service deals.

powerStructure.name: John Laurent
powerStructure.title: State CIO, OA-ITSD
powerStructure.note: Named Missouri CIO in Dec 2024 after serving as Acting CIO (from May 2023) and Deputy CIO; previously Global VP of IT at Enterprise Holdings. The top relationship for the consolidated executive-branch IT motion.

powerStructure.name: Ken Zellers
powerStructure.title: Commissioner, Office of Administration
powerStructure.note: Leads OA (ITSD's parent) and is tasked by EO 26-02 with building the statewide AI framework — a senior sponsor for AI and enterprise-modernization conversations.

powerStructure.name: ITSD Information Security office
powerStructure.title: Enterprise cybersecurity / CISO function
powerStructure.note: Owns executive-branch security policy and operations. Confirm the current CISO by name before engaging — the role has turned over in recent years; lead through the security office.

powerStructure.name: OA Division of Purchasing
powerStructure.title: Statewide IT contracting
powerStructure.note: Establishes statewide contracts, runs MissouriBUYS and the Cooperative Procurement Program — the contractual path onto state and cooperative vehicles.

news.title: Gov. Kehoe signs Executive Order 26-02 directing a statewide AI framework
news.impact: OA Commissioner Zellers tasked to deliver an AI integration framework by Nov 30, 2026 — opens AI-infrastructure, secure-data-platform, and responsible-AI conversations with ITSD as the delivery arm.

news.title: Missouri secures ~$15B Google data-center investment in New Florence
news.impact: Largest single private investment in state history; cements Missouri as an AI-infrastructure market and a state energy/policy priority — strong framing for AI-infrastructure positioning.

news.title: Gov. Kehoe signs SB 4, setting data-center cost-recovery and utility rules
news.impact: Large-load data centers must cover 100% of their power and infrastructure costs — shapes the energy economics behind Missouri's data-center boom and AI-infrastructure narrative.

news.title: John Laurent named permanent Missouri State CIO
news.impact: Settled leadership at the top of the consolidated IT motion after an interim period — confirm/build the top relationship.

dellActions: Confirm Dell's coverage on Missouri statewide contracts (MissouriBUYS) and NASPO ValuePoint participating addenda, and verify MissouriBUYS vendor registration — the access path to ITSD and, via the Cooperative Procurement Program, the broader Missouri public sector.
Lead ISG and CSG into ITSD's consolidated infrastructure, network, and end-user refresh across the ~15 executive departments it delivers for — a single centralized buying environment.

Tie AI-infrastructure and secure-data-platform value to EO 26-02's statewide AI framework; engage OA Commissioner Zellers and CIO Laurent as the framework takes shape ahead of the Nov 30, 2026 deadline.
Use the Google New Florence build and SB 4 as market context to frame Missouri as an AI-infrastructure priority — without conflating private data-center investment with ITSD's own buying.

Engage ITSD's Information Security office on enterprise cybersecurity, and track SLCGP grant flow (via the Dept. of Public Safety) reaching county, city, and K-12 buyers; confirm the current CISO by name before forecasting the security buying center.
